title: [SV] ETC crash with signed printf operand
body:
Input:
```firrtl
FIRRTL version 5.1.0
circuit SignedPrintETC :

  public module SignedPrintETC :
    input clk : Clock
    input test : SInt<64>

    printf(clk, UInt<1>(0h1), "%0d", test)
```

When using `--extract-test-code` this produces invalid IR:

```
signed-print-etc.fir:4:10: error: operand #0 does not dominate this use
  public module SignedPrintETC :
         ^
signed-print-etc.fir:4:10: note: see current operation: "hw.instance"(%2, %1, %0) <{argNames = ["port_0", "port_1", "port_2"], doNotPrint, inner_sym = #hw<innerSym@__ETC_SignedPrintETC_assert>, instanceName = "SignedPrintETC_assert", moduleName = @SignedPrintETC_assert, parameters = [], resultNames = []}> : (i64, i1, i1) -> ()
signed-print-etc.fir:8:5: note: operand defined here (op in a child region)
    printf(clk, UInt<1>(0h1), "%0d", test)
    ^
```
code_blocks:
FIRRTL version 5.1.0
circuit SignedPrintETC :

  public module SignedPrintETC :
    input clk : Clock
    input test : SInt<64>

    printf(clk, UInt<1>(0h1), "%0d", test)
signed-print-etc.fir:4:10: error: operand #0 does not dominate this use
  public module SignedPrintETC :
         ^
signed-print-etc.fir:4:10: note: see current operation: "hw.instance"(%2, %1, %0) <{argNames = ["port_0", "port_1", "port_2"], doNotPrint, inner_sym = #hw<innerSym@__ETC_SignedPrintETC_assert>, instanceName = "SignedPrintETC_assert", moduleName = @SignedPrintETC_assert, parameters = [], resultNames = []}> : (i64, i1, i1) -> ()
signed-print-etc.fir:8:5: note: operand defined here (op in a child region)
    printf(clk, UInt<1>(0h1), "%0d", test)
    ^
